About This Project

Built for Loudoun.
By the Community.

The Loudoun County Resource Hub is a Technology Student Association Webmaster competition entry — and a genuinely useful tool for Loudoun County residents who need help finding local services.

Our Mission

Why We Built This

Loudoun County is one of the fastest-growing counties in the United States, yet many residents — especially newcomers, seniors, and families facing hardship — struggle to find the community services available to them. Resources exist, but they're scattered across dozens of different websites, phone books, and word-of-mouth networks.

The Loudoun County Resource Hub solves that problem by bringing every major Loudoun County resource into one clean, searchable, always-accessible directory. Whether you need food assistance, mental health support, youth programs, or emergency housing, this hub puts the answer one search away.

Every resource listed has been individually researched and verified. We include real phone numbers, addresses, and direct website links so residents can take action immediately — not just read about services in the abstract.

32+ Resources Listed

Individually researched organizations serving Loudoun County

6 Categories

Food, Health, Youth, Housing, Support Services, and Events

24/7 Always Online

Hosted on GitHub Pages, accessible any time, no login required

100% Free to Use

No ads, no paywalls, no tracking — just helpful information

How It Works

Simple by Design

We built this site with one goal: get residents to the help they need as fast as possible.

Search Instantly

Type any keyword into the search bar — a resource name, a service type, a neighborhood — and the directory updates in real time without reloading the page.

Filter by Category

Use the category filter buttons to narrow the directory to just one type of service — Food, Health, Youth, Housing, Support, or Community Events.

Contact Directly

Every resource card shows a phone number, address, and direct website link. No middlemen — connect with the organization directly from the directory.

Submit New Resources

Know of a resource we're missing? Use the submission form on the home page to suggest additions. We review every submission within 48 hours.

Technology

How We Built It

Built entirely from scratch using modern web standards — no templates, no pre-built themes.

HTML5

Semantic, accessible markup using proper landmark elements, ARIA attributes, and WCAG-compliant structure throughout.

CSS3

Modern CSS with custom properties (variables), Flexbox, CSS Grid, smooth transitions, and a full dark mode system — all without any CSS framework.

Vanilla JavaScript

ES6 modules power the live search, category filtering, IntersectionObserver animations, form validation, and dark mode — no frameworks required.

Vite + GitHub Pages

Vite provides a lightning-fast local development server and optimized production builds. GitHub Pages hosts the site live, 24/7, for free.

Mobile-First Design

Every layout is designed for mobile first, then enhanced for tablets and desktops using CSS media queries and responsive grid systems.

WCAG Accessibility

Skip links, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, high contrast ratios, and screen reader support are built into every component on the site.

Competition Context

TSA Webmaster Event

This website was created as an entry for the Technology Student Association (TSA) High School Webmaster competitive event for the 2025–2026 school year. The TSA Webmaster event challenges student teams to design, build, and launch a website addressing a given annual theme.

The 2025–2026 theme is the Community Resource Hub — a directory highlighting resources available to residents of a specific community. Our team chose Loudoun County, Virginia as our focus area, researching and documenting over 30 real, verified local organizations.

All web pages were developed during the current school year. All sources are cited on the References page. The Student Copyright Checklist and Work Log are linked there as well.

21st Century Skills Demonstrated

  • Critical Thinking & Problem Solving
  • Creativity & Innovation
  • Communication
  • Collaboration & Teamwork
  • Initiative & Leadership
  • Dependability & Integrity
  • Flexibility & Adaptability
